title: Second cancer-site explainer pack
context: After M6 (sustain and refresh), the project may expand to a second cancer site. This is a backlog task: it is only started after the first site is stable, the ops runbook (incidence-explorers-ops-018) is operational, and the expansion process (documented in ops-018) is followed. Like the pilot site, any patient-facing explainer for a second cancer site requires full provenance and oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off before shipping. No second site, reviewer, or partner is yet identified.
objective: Apply the full incidence-explorers pipeline to a second cancer site (to be selected using the same scored criteria as the pilot): ingest aggregate data with provenance, validate against the upstream tool, produce accessible dashboards with caveats, write a provenanced 'not medical advice'-framed explainer with verified helplines, obtain oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off, and ship through the documented expert-gated expansion process from incidence-explorers-ops-018.
acceptanceCriteria: The second cancer site is selected using the same scored criteria as the pilot site (public-misinterpretation value, data availability, reviewer availability, tractable complexity) and the gated expansion process from incidence-explorers-ops-018 is followed
Full provenance is carried on every DataPoint for the second site; allow-list, suppression, and license-register gates are all green
Accessible dashboards with inline caveats (WCAG 2.2 AA) are produced for the second site
The explainer is provenanced, 'not medical advice'-framed, uses verified/dated helplines, passes the content-safety lint, and meets the plain-language readability target
Signed off by a credentialed oncologist AND a patient advocate (version-scoped, recorded) before shipping — blocking
output: A second cancer-site explainer pack (dashboards + explainer + verified helplines) — fully provenanced, 'not medical advice'-framed, WCAG 2.2 AA, oncologist + advocate signed off — delivered through the expert-gated expansion process.
